op_code_info.text: Play anim {} and wait for it to end
op_code_info.comment: Queue model animation (section 3 id = opcode) and pause the sequence until it finishes

op_code_info.text: Step dynamic texture anim {} (section 4): advance one frame, or scroll the texture in VRAM if nb_destination is 0
op_code_info.comment: Uses DynamicTextureData byte6 as speed (1/8th frame units) and byte7 as counter

op_code_info.text: Restore hidden model part {} (clears hidden-part bit, 15-frame re-attach effect)
op_code_info.comment: Bit set/cleared via E5 0x20; see also A6

op_code_info.text: Set combat flag 0x40 (unknown)
Load battle stage camera/geometry (command flags {})
op_code_info.comment: BS_loadCameraAndGeometry: bit0 = camera, bit1 = geometry

op_code_info.text: Start sine-wave vertex animation (model wobble) with param {}
Hide Selection Cursor
op_code_info.comment: Clears the selection-triangle flag (86 sets it)

op_code_info.text: Show Selection Cursor
op_code_info.comment: Sets the selection-triangle flag (85 clears it)

op_code_info.text: Multi-part monster link {}: value<3 unlink whole chain, 3-15 link self to that slot, >=16 link self to the monster with that com_id
op_code_info.comment: Linked entities form a circular chain: sequences start on every part simultaneously and parts copy the chain head's transform (e.g. Ultimecia final form)

op_code_info.text: Print monster text {} with param {}
op_code_info.comment: Urgent text channel

op_code_info.text: Setup targeting context: {} (value<7: target = that slot, attacker unchanged; >=7: attacker = self, keep target mask - typical at the start of an attack). Recomputes the distance-based speed factors (C3 0x10/0x11), target slot (C3 0x18), attacker-target angle (C3 0x1A) and rotates the attacker to face the target. Multi-target: uses the centre of all targets
Trigger sequence {1} on entity {0} (slot id if <16, else on every loaded entity with com_id {0})
op_code_info.comment: Size was previously wrongly documented as 3; the game consumes 2 bytes

op_code_info.text: Toggle Shadow
Reset Position (X and Z)
Camera shake: amplitude from {} to {} over {} frames (sign alternates each frame)
op_code_info.comment: Writes BATTLE_CAMERA_SHAKE_OFFSET (earthquake effect)

op_code_info.text: Play sound general audio list with sound id from category: 0, sound id: 31, channel mask selection: {} and {}
Play sound general audio list with sound id from category: 0, sound id: 31, channel mask selection: {} and {}
Queue walk effect and sound
op_code_info.comment: Infinite param list ending with FF

op_code_info.text: Set background sequence id {} (re-run from its start every frame before the main sequence; per-frame logic only, must not queue animations)
op_code_info.comment: Cleared by writing 0; skipped while combat flag 0x08 is set

op_code_info.text: Change texture using texture anim id {} with destination index being the parameters of C3:{}
op_code_info.comment: Second param goes through the C3 special-value reader

op_code_info.text: Show back (turn around)
op_code_info.comment: combat_flags |= TURN_BACK

op_code_info.text: Mark self as dead/removed from battle (bookkeeping)
op_code_info.comment: Sets flag 0x40 + status_1 bit0 in battle slot data and calls setMaskEnable

op_code_info.text: Move smoothly toward entity slot {} with speed param {}
op_code_info.comment: Task moveEntityToTargetSmoothly

op_code_info.text: Blink timeline: toggle texture anim 0 on/off, each byte = frame delay before next toggle, FF ends the list
op_code_info.comment: Infinite param list ending with FF; task auto-ends when the sequence changes

op_code_info.text: Play anim {} without pausing the sequence (following opcodes keep running; left as-is if already playing unless the anim-finished flag is set)
op_code_info.comment: Opcode < 0x80 pauses until the anim ends; A0 does not

op_code_info.text: Yield: pause the sequence for this frame (resume here next frame)
End of sequence: reset rotation and jump to the next queued/auto sequence
Set current seq as base seq; jump to pending next seq if it exists, else set continue flag
Set the anim-finished flag (0x4): forces the next A0 to restart the animation even if it is already playing
op_code_info.comment: The flag is also set automatically when an animation reaches its last frame

op_code_info.text: Effect ID {}
op_code_info.comment: Param forced to 1 (GF) when battle flag 0x40000000 is set

op_code_info.text: Detach model part {0} (section 2 object index) and make it fly off ballistically with initial velocity ({1}, {2}, {3}): velocity decays 1/8 per frame (drag), +100/frame added on Y (gravity), for 15 frames. Param4 {4} stored in the task (unidentified). The part is flagged in the hidden-parts bitmask (E5 0x20); opcode 81 restores it
op_code_info.comment: Body parts falling/flying off, e.g. Gerogero

op_code_info.text: Jump to sequence {} (goto, no return)
op_code_info.comment: Sets the instruction pointer to the start of the sequence and updates currentAnimSeqId

op_code_info.text: Fade effect ID {}
End sequence (hard stop): bake current transforms and stop for this frame (next sequence resolved next frame; A2 chains immediately instead)
op_code_info.comment: TerminateSequenceAndUpdateTransforms + stop interpreter

op_code_info.text: Apply the pending action result to ALL targets at once: show damage/heal numbers, apply status changes (with visual side effects), queue the targets' hit/death animations, handle counter-attacker. Use B2/B7 instead to spread results over a multi-hit choreography

Renzokuken init (character weapons): pre-scans the following opcodes until A1 without executing them, summing the frame count of each anim opcode (<0x80) into buckets split at each A0. The per-stage durations drive the R1-trigger timing bar; execution then resumes normally
op_code_info.comment: Timing windows are computed from the actual animation lengths of the slashes

op_code_info.text: Restore base model (undo model swap) and chain to sequence {} (0 = auto)
op_code_info.comment: Also restores the weapon com file; ends the sequence

op_code_info.text: Drag/carry the target: stick it to attacker bone {0} (FF = pin at target's own base position) with bone interp {1} (0-4096 along the bone), for {2} frames. Shorthand for AE with the midpoint bone forced to FF
op_code_info.comment: Grab moves; target shadow hidden during the drag, restored after (position is NOT auto-reset - the snap-back reaction does that on hit)

op_code_info.text: Drag/carry the target (grab moves): each frame for {3} frames, move the target so it sticks to attacker bone {0} (FF = pin at target's own base position; bit7 selects the target reference point). Bone {1} (unless FF): anchor at the midpoint between the two bones (e.g. between jaws). Interp {2} = 0-4096 position along the bone
op_code_info.comment: Target shadow hidden during the drag, restored after; position NOT auto-reset (snap-back reaction handles it on hit)

op_code_info.text: Trigger sequence {} on the current target

Spawn hit/impact particle effect on the ATTACKER (sparks/dust/flash when a blow lands). Byte1 = effect id, byte2 = flag byte (spawn at bone / random bone / custom position / default). Always shown, even on a miss (contrast with B4)
op_code_info.comment: Flag-based param count, see B0/B4 parse

op_code_info.text: Queue walk effect and sound (SFX variant)
op_code_info.comment: Infinite param list ending with FF

op_code_info.text: Apply the action result (damage/status/hit anim) to the current target, then advance to the NEXT target: everything referring to 'current target' (BF, B4, AD/AE, AF, C3 0x18...) now points to the next victim of the multi-target attack

Impact on the TARGET: same encoding as B0 but the particle effect spawns on the target's model. First applies the snap-back reaction if flagged (dragged target teleported back + plays seq 22); the whole visual is skipped when the attack missed. Standard 'blow connects' opcode, paired with BF
op_code_info.comment: Params still consumed on a miss (except the bone byte of flag 0x08)

op_code_info.text: Play sound from character/monster with sound id: {}, channel mask selection: {} and {}
Play sound from character/monster with sound id: {}, channel mask selection: {} and {}
Apply the action result (damage/status/hit anim) to the current target WITHOUT advancing to the next one (contrast with B2): the sequence can keep acting on the same target afterwards
Play sound general audio list with sound id from category: {}, sound id: {}, channel mask selection: {} and {}
op_code_info.comment: Plays the miss sound instead when the attack missed

op_code_info.text: Wait {} - 1 frames before continuing
op_code_info.comment: Sets delayBeforeNextAnimation and pauses the interpreter

op_code_info.text: Advance current animation by one frame
Print attack name
op_code_info.comment: Only if a battle text is pending

op_code_info.text: Nop (param {} ignored)
Change texture using texture anim id {} with destination index {}
Stop texture anim 0 and set GF-hidden flag to {}
op_code_info.comment: Flag hides the model while a GF is summoned

op_code_info.text: Moment of impact: make the target flinch by triggering sequence {} on it (a sequence id in the TARGET's dat, usually its take-a-hit flinch). Does nothing if the attack missed, a snap-back reaction is pending, or the target is defending/invincible/not interruptible. Only plays the animation - damage/status are applied by AA/B2/B7
op_code_info.comment: Typical use: right after each hit of a multi-hit attack

special_change_current_value_params.text: If target is self, speed_factor = 1000, else speed_factor = 5000*distance/4096
If target is self, speed_factor = 1000, else speed_factor = speed from 0x10 - (2000*(attacker_speed_factor + target_speed_factor)/4096).
Weapon anim data word at +28 (set via E5 0x14)
Weapon anim data word at +30 (set via E5 0x15)
Weapon anim data word at +32 (set via E5 0x16)
Attack hit flag 2 of current target (hitType2 & 2)
Angle between the target and the attacker & 0xFFF
If monster: 2048, else if based rotation Y & 0xFFFu <= 2048 return 0 Else return 4096
Camera flag (also readable from camera sequences)
1 if GF summon data is loaded, else 0
1 if another entity is alive and free (not dead/stopped/petrified), else 0
Y offset of target's effect-spawn point 0xF1 above its base Y
Y offset of target's effect-spawn point 0xF0 above its base Y

e5_special_params.text: Hide/show model part: value>0 hides object (value-1), value<0 shows object (-1-value)
Camera var of the entity (readable from camera sequences via camera-C3 0x15)

fade_effect_id.text: Slowly fade out (but fade back in suddenly at the end)
Fade out and leave combat (no EXP given)

sound_channel_flag.text: volume = linked to current entity position
